description:
Automated translation (refer to source for accuracy)

On April 1, 2026 (Reiwa 8), sections of National Route 299 within Hanno City will be discontinued and transferred to Hanno City roads and prefectural roads.

【Transfer to Hanno City roads】
The section from Hanno Station Front Intersection to the border with Iruma City will become Hanno City Road 1st District No. 2,961. Requests for road occupancy permits, road construction approval applications, and road boundary certificates should now be submitted to Hanno City Hall instead.

【Change to prefectural roads】
The section from Nakayama Overpass (West) Intersection to Hanno Station Front Intersection will become a prefectural road, continuing to be managed by Saitama Prefecture (Hanno Regional Maintenance Office). Within this section, the stretch from Nakayama Overpass (West) Intersection to Higashimachi Intersection will be designated as the Major Local Road Ome-Hanno Line, and from Higashimachi Intersection to Hanno Station Front Intersection will be the General Prefectural Road Hanno Station Line.

【Other changes】
The section from Noda, Iruma City to Sasai, Sayama City will be transferred to Iruma City roads and Sayama City roads respectively.

For details, contact the Hanno Regional Maintenance Office (042-973-2281) or the Kawagoe Regional Maintenance Office (049-243-2020).

Source (Japanese): https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/tosikensetubu/ijikanrika/12982.html
summary: ℹ️ National Route 299 sections in Hanno to transfer to city and prefectural roads on April 1, 2026 (Reiwa 8)
